results.raw_output:
To close the context menu in the screenshot, you can do one of two things:

1.  **Press the Escape key:** This is a standard method for closing any open context menu in Windows Explorer and will immediately close the menu at the bottom right of the screen.

2.  **Click anywhere outside the context menu:** You can also click on an empty area of the screen (such as the blue background or another window) to close the context menu.

Both methods should achieve the same result—closing the context menu without selecting anything from it.

To minimize the current window, you need to interact with the close control at the top right corner of the window. Click on the small "X" button located there. This is a standard UI element for closing or minimizing windows in most operating systems.

The goal is to play the "Microsoft Solitaire Collection" game, as shown in the image. To do this, I need to click on the prominent card image of the Solitaire Collection at the top left corner of the screen. This will start the game and allow me to begin playing. The interface clearly displays the game tile with its logo and a "Play" button next to it, making it easy to select.

To open the gallery, you need to click on the "Photos" icon in the lower right corner of the screen. This is a common location for the Photos app shortcut on Windows 10 and Windows 11. The user's goal is to access the gallery feature within this app, which will allow them to view their photos. Therefore, clicking on the "Photos" icon is the correct step.

The screenshot shows the PowerPoint drawing toolbar. The "Style" section at the bottom right corner of this toolbar contains several options for adjusting the style of the selected object, such as Fill, Outline, Style, etc. 

To adjust the style (fill color, outline, or other stylistic features) of the currently selected shape, you would typically click on one of these style-related buttons in the Style dropdown menu. For example, to change the fill color, you would click on the "Fill" button. To change the outline, you would click on the "Outline" button, and so on.

Therefore, based on what is shown in the image, if you want to adjust the style of the selected object, your next step should be to interact with the Style dropdown menu.
